plan: Identify the exact make and model of the camera currently in possession by checking the box, receipt, or documentation that came with it.
Note all current camera specifications (sensor size, resolution, aperture, battery type, weight, connectivity, etc.) to use as a matching baseline — everything except zoom must remain the same.
Record the current zoom specification (e.g., 30x optical zoom) as the baseline to be exceeded.
Research the full product line from the same manufacturer to find cameras that share all the same specifications but offer the highest available optical zoom capacity.
Identify the specific replacement model with maximum zoom that satisfies all other spec constraints.
Calculate the price difference between the original camera and the replacement model.
Contact the retailer or manufacturer (phone, chat, or in-store — whichever is fastest) to initiate an exchange request, citing the zoom capacity mismatch with intended use (bird-watching).
Confirm the retailer's exchange policy (restocking fees, condition requirements, return window) and ensure eligibility.
Select the fastest available exchange method — in-store exchange in San Antonio (ZIP 78260) is likely quickest; if not available locally, request expedited shipping.
Confirm that PayPal is accepted as a payment method for any price difference, and complete the additional payment via PayPal at checkout.
Obtain confirmation of the exchange order and estimated delivery or pickup date.
Upon receiving the new camera, verify all specifications match (except zoom) and that the zoom capacity is as expected for bird-watching use.

In a real execution of this plan, the price difference would be determined as follows: (1) look up the MSRP or purchase price of the original camera from the receipt or retailer account, (2) look up the current retail price of the identified replacement model with maximum zoom but otherwise identical specs, and (3) subtract the original price from the replacement price to get the amount owed. For example, if the original camera was $400 and the replacement superzoom model is $500, the additional payment via PayPal would be $100. This calculation must be confirmed with the retailer at the time of the exchange request, as prices may vary and the retailer may apply a trade-in credit rather than a straight price difference.
